item.title: Fijiana begin Bordeaux 7s Campaign with win over Japan
item.description: The Fiji Airways Fijiana National 7s team opened their Bordeaux 7s campaign in France with a convincing 24-12 victory over Japan in their first Pool B match. Atelaite Ralivanawa starred in the opening half, scoring two tries to help Fiji take control of the contest.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Fijiana National 7s team opened their Bordeaux 7s campaign in France with a convincing 24-12 victory over Japan in their first Pool B match.

Atelaite Ralivanawa starred in the opening half, scoring two tries to help Fiji take control of the contest.

She crossed the line in the second minute to give Fijiana an early lead.

Japan responded with a try of their own to level the scores, but an unsuccessful conversion left the match finely poised. Ralivanawa then grabbed her second try in the sixth minute, extending Fiji’s advantage to 12-5 at halftime.

In the second half, Reapi Ulunisau added two more tries to strengthen Fiji’s lead.

Japan managed just one additional try, but it was not enough to threaten the Fijiana side as they secured a 24-12 win.

Fijiana will face Australia at 3:51am tomorrow before taking on Brazil at 11:32pm in their final Pool B match.

The women’s Cup quarter-finals are scheduled to begin at 1:30am on Sunday, followed by the semi-finals at 10:30pm later that day.

The tournament final will be played at 3:04am on Monday.

item.title: A severe Hurricane warning issued
item.description: Fiji-born Kini Naholo's Hurricanes side has issued a warning to other teams in the Super Rugby Pacific Competition after the Canes thumped the Brumbies 66-12 in Wellington. Any questions over whether the Hurricanes were the real deal in 2026 have been answered in emphatic fashion.
item.content:encoded:
Fiji-born Kini Naholo's Hurricanes side has issued a warning to other teams in the Super Rugby Pacific Competition after the Canes thumped the Brumbies 66-12 in Wellington.

Any questions over whether the Hurricanes were the real deal in 2026 have been answered in emphatic fashion.

The onslaught started early, and the foot was not taken off the throat as the top qualifiers sent an ominous warning to the competition.

Ngane Punivai bagged a trio of late tries while Ruben Love kicked a perfect 10 from ten off the tee.

Striking twice inside the opening ten minutes, the Canes set the tone quickly.

Despite the wet, the Hurricanes threw caution to the wind with some expansive play, running the Brumbies ragged in a dominant display.

The opener went to Caleb Delany, capping off some cracking interplay between the backs.

Brumbies mistakes were swiftly punished through clinical counter attack, Asafo Aumua with a beautiful ball in the tackle to Josh Moorby who linked with Cam Roigard running a perfect support line.

Next it was Love to break the line, sending Naholo down the touch-line with the final inside ball to Billy Proctor.

Roigard bagged a brace before the break with Warner Dearns also getting on the score-sheet as the Hurricanes took a commanding 38-0 half-time lead.

It took ten minutes for the Canes to score after oranges, Dearns dotting down for his second.

The replacement winger got the first of his three as he pounced on a loose Brumbies pass, and set off from halfway, Wright closed in but Punivai did just enough to get it on the grass.

His second came off the back of some set piece perfection.

A genius switch play at the lineout sent Punivai in again untouched before he completed his trio after Love sent a a picture perfect kick across, Punivai powering to the line to bring curtains on the demolition job.

item.title: Luke Jacobson eyes All Blacks return after ‘frustrating’ 2025 with no playing time in tests
item.description: Luke Jacobson was “frustrated” as a combination of bad luck and being overlooked denied him any playing time in the All Blacks’ tr

item.title: Tuimaba returns for Fiji in final World Series tournament in France
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026 Olympic gold medallist Aminiasi Tuimaba will once again fly the Fijian flag on the international sevens stage after being named in the Fiji Airways Fiji Men's National 7s squad for the final tournament of the World 7s Championship in France.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026

Olympic gold medallist Aminiasi Tuimaba will once again fly the Fijian flag on the international sevens stage after being named in the Fiji Airways Fiji Men's National 7s squad for the final tournament of the World 7s Championship in France.

Tuimaba is expected to bring a wealth of experience to the side and will be one of the key players to watch in Bordeaux.

Other players named in the squad are Jeremaia Matana, Kavekini Tanivanuakula, Joseva Talacolo, Isaia Rugu, George Bose, Terio Veilawa, Filipe Sauturaga, Apete Narogo, Vuiviawa Naduvalo, Nacani Boginisoko, Sakiusa Siqila and Tomasi Vuluma.

The Fiji Men's 7s side will open its campaign against Kenya at 12.44am tomorrow before taking on Great Britain at 6.14am in its second pool match.

They will then face South Africa at 9.06pm tomorrow in their final pool game.

The men's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 3.28am on Sunday, while the semi-finals are scheduled for 11.14pm on Sunday. The final will kick off at 3.41am on Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fijiana 7s side will open its Bordeaux 7s campaign against Japan at 10.04pm tonight.

They will then face Australia at 3.51am tomorrow before taking on Brazil at 11.32pm tomorrow in their final pool match.

The women's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 1.30am on Sunday, while the semi-finals are scheduled for 10.30pm on Sunday. The final will be played at 3.04am on Monday.

item.title: Super Rugby Pacific CEO Mesley defends finals format amid predictability claims
item.description: The Super Rugby Pacific playoffs have kicked off amid sold-out stadiums, high demand for tickets and debate over the competition’s finals format. Christchurch’s One NZ Stadium at Te Kaha has already sold out for the Crusaders’ qualifying final against the Blues, with tickets snapped up in around 20 minutes.
item.content:encoded:
The Super Rugby Pacific playoffs have kicked off amid sold-out stadiums, high demand for tickets and debate over the competition’s finals format.

Christchurch’s One NZ Stadium at Te Kaha has already sold out for the Crusaders’ qualifying final against the Blues, with tickets snapped up in around 20 minutes.

The fast sell-out reflects the intensity of interest in the all-Kiwi clash, which headlines a finals series dominated by New Zealand sides.

Four New Zealand teams: the Hurricanes, the Blues, the Crusaders and the Chiefs, have all finished inside the top six, setting up a strong Kiwi presence in the knockout stages.

Despite ongoing criticism of the “lucky loser” format, Super Rugby Pacific officials have opted to retain the system for this year’s finals series.

Under the structure, the highest-ranked losing team from the qualifying finals will still progress to the semi-finals, a rule that has divided opinion among fans and commentators.

Super Rugby Pacific CEO Jack Mesley says while some believe the outcome of the competition may appear predictable given the dominance of New Zealand teams, he does not agree.

He pointed to last year’s finals as evidence of the competition’s unpredictability, noting that the sixth-placed Blues defeated the top-ranked Chiefs in the qualifying final and came close to eliminating the Crusaders in the semi-finals.

Mesley says those results demonstrate that no team can be ruled out once the playoffs begin, and that past performances show the finals can still deliver surprises despite early expectations.

The Brumbies will take on the Hurricanes at 7.05pm tonight.

In other qualifying finals, the Crusaders will host the Blues at 4.35pm tomorrow, while the Chiefs will meet the Reds at 7.05pm tomorrow.

item.title: Switzerland's Embolo gets green light for World Cup travel
item.description: Switzerland forward Breel Embolo has been cleared to travel to the United States after his Esta authorisation was approved, allowing him to join his teammates ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Embolo had been unable to travel with the squad earlier this week after his application was placed under review due to a Swiss court ruling linked to a 2018 altercation in Basel.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland forward Breel Embolo has been cleared to travel to the United States after his Esta authorisation was approved, allowing him to join his teammates ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

Embolo had been unable to travel with the squad earlier this week after his application was placed under review due to a Swiss court ruling linked to a 2018 altercation in Basel.

The Switzerland Football Federation confirmed that the 29-year-old is expected to join the team on Friday.

Embolo was convicted in 2023 of making multiple threats and received a suspended fine. He later chose not to appeal the ruling further, making the judgement final.

Embolo has scored 23 goals in 85 appearances for Switzerland and played at the 2018 and 2022 World Cups.

Switzerland will begin its FIFA World Cup campaign against Qatar on June 14th before facing Bosnia and Herzegovina on June 19th and Canada on June 25th.

Meanwhile, the FIFA World Cup will kick off next Friday and will run until July 20th.

item.title: NBA Finals: Jalen Brunson stars as New York Knicks beat San Antonio Spurs in Game 1
item.description: The New York Knicks raised their NBA Playoffs winning streak to a dozen games as Jalen Brunson starred in their Finals opener agai

item.title: Fijiana 7s eye strong start against Japan in Bordeaux tonight
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side will be looking for a strong start against Japan in the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Series in France tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side will be looking for a strong start against Japan in the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Series in France tonight.

Speaking from France, coach Richard Walker says they played Japan in Hong Kong, but failed to stick to their game plan, which resulted in a loss.

He says they cannot afford to be overconfident against the Japanese side, as they are a very disciplined team.

Walker says success will come down to discipline and ensuring the players support one another on the field.

The coach says one of the biggest lessons the team has learned throughout the season is the importance of consistency.

Insert: Richard Walker on consistency – 5 June 2026

The Fijiana 7s side is currently seventh on the World Rugby Sevens Series standings with 16 points.

Australia leads the standings with 38 points, followed by New Zealand with 36 points, while the USA sits third with 30 points.

The Fijiana will open their Bordeaux 7s campaign against Japan at 10.04 tonight. They will then face Australia at 3.51am tomorrow before taking on Brazil at 11.32pm in their final pool match.

The women's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 1.30am on Sunday, while the semi-finals are scheduled for 10.30pm on Sunday. The final will be played at 3.04am on Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Men's 7s side will open its campaign against Kenya at 12.44am tomorrow before meeting Great Britain at 6.14am in its second pool match.

They will then face South Africa at 9.06pm in their final pool game.

The men's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 3.28am on Sunday, the semi-finals are scheduled for 11.14pm on Sunday, and the final will kick off at 3.41am on Monday.

item.description: There is no place for drugs in schools or in the Vodafone Deans Trophy competition this season.That was the clear message from Fij

item.description: Results will not be the primary focus for Bula Boys coach Stéphane Auvray as the team prepares for two FIFA international friendli
